words.definitionEn: a short piece of writing by a student as part of a course of study
words.exampleEn: I have to write an essay this weekend.

words.definitionEn: used or happening every day or regularly; ordinary
words.exampleEn: Change is a part of everyday life in business.

words.definitionEn: in, to or at every place; all places
words.exampleEn: I've looked everywhere.

words.definitionEn: to let somebody/something do something; to let something happen or be done
words.exampleEn: allow somebody/something to do something, His parents won't allow him to stay out late.

words.definitionEn: the facts, signs or objects that make you believe that something is true
words.exampleEn: evidence (of something), Researchers have found clear scientific evidence of a link between exposure to sun and skin cancer.

words.definitionEn: correct in every detail
words.exampleEn: The new palace is an exact replica of the original building.

words.definitionEn: used to emphasize that something is correct in every way or in every detail
words.exampleEn: It's exactly nine o'clock.

words.definitionEn: extremely good
words.exampleEn: an excellent book/article

words.definitionEn: used before you mention the only thing or person about which a statement is not true
words.exampleEn: We work every day except Sunday.

words.definitionEn: not quite
words.exampleEn: I like almost all of them.

words.definitionEn: without any other people
words.exampleEn: not alone in, Tom is not alone in finding Rick hard to work with.

words.definitionEn: to be real; to be present in a place or situation
words.exampleEn: Does life exist on other planets?

words.definitionEn: without any other people
words.exampleEn: not alone in, Tom is not alone in finding Rick hard to work with.

words.definitionEn: to think or believe that something will happen or that somebody will do something
words.exampleEn: expect something, The company is expecting record sales this year.

words.definitionEn: forward
words.exampleEn: I was just walking along singing to myself.

words.definitionEn: the knowledge and skill that you have gained through doing something for a period of time; the process of gaining this
words.exampleEn: My lack of practical experience was a disadvantage.

words.definitionEn: a scientific test that is done in order to study what happens and to gain new knowledge
words.exampleEn: to do/perform/conduct an experiment

words.definitionEn: done with, having or involving great knowledge or skill
words.exampleEn: to seek expert advice

words.definitionEn: a person with special knowledge, skill or training in something
words.exampleEn: a legal/security/medical/computer expert

words.definitionEn: from one end to or towards the other end of something
words.exampleEn: They walked slowly along the road.

words.definitionEn: a statement, fact, or situation that tells you why something happened; a reason given for something
words.exampleEn: The most likely explanation is that his plane was delayed.

words.definitionEn: to show or make known a feeling, an opinion, etc. by words, looks or actions
words.exampleEn: express something, Teachers have expressed concern about the changes.

words.definitionEn: a word or phrase
words.exampleEn: What's the meaning of the expression ‘on cloud nine’?

words.definitionEn: before now or before a particular time in the past
words.exampleEn: ‘Lunch?’ ‘No thanks, I've already eaten.’

words.definitionEn: very great in degree
words.exampleEn: We are working under extreme pressure at the moment.

words.definitionEn: to a very high degree
words.exampleEn: It is extremely important to follow the directions exactly.

words.definitionEn: one of several things that cause or influence something
words.exampleEn: Obesity is a major risk factor for heart disease.

words.definitionEn: a building or group of buildings where goods are made, mainly by machine
words.exampleEn: a car factory

words.definitionEn: to not be successful in achieving something
words.exampleEn: Many diets fail because they are boring.

words.definitionEn: the fact that somebody/something is able to do something
words.exampleEn: People with the disease may lose their ability to communicate.

words.definitionEn: acceptable and appropriate in a particular situation
words.exampleEn: a fair deal/wage/price

words.definitionEn: an act of falling
words.exampleEn: I had a bad fall and broke my arm.

words.definitionEn: a thing that you can choose to do or have out of two or more possibilities
words.exampleEn: The car is too expensive so we're trying to find a cheaper alternative.

words.definitionEn: a person who admires somebody/something or enjoys watching or listening to somebody/something very much
words.exampleEn: sports/music fans

words.definitionEn: used for introducing a statement that makes the main statement in a sentence seem surprising
words.exampleEn: Although the sun was shining, it wasn't very warm.

words.definitionEn: to use land for growing crops and/or keeping animals
words.exampleEn: The family has farmed in Kent for over two hundred years.

words.definitionEn: the business of managing or working on a farm
words.exampleEn: to take up farming

words.definitionEn: a popular style of clothes, hair, etc. at a particular time or place; the state of being popular
words.exampleEn: the latest fashion trends

words.definitionEn: a white or yellow substance in the bodies of animals and humans, stored under the skin
words.exampleEn: excess body fat

words.definitionEn: the bad feeling that you have when you are in danger or when a particular thing frightens you
words.exampleEn: Her eyes showed no fear.

words.definitionEn: something important, interesting or typical of a place or thing
words.exampleEn: An interesting feature of the city is the old market.

words.definitionEn: to give food to a person or an animal
words.exampleEn: feed somebody/something/yourself, Have you fed the cat yet?

words.definitionEn: being a woman or a girl
words.exampleEn: a female student/employee/artist/athlete

words.definitionEn: an animal that can lay eggs or give birth to babies; a plant that can produce fruit
words.exampleEn: One adult female can lay 400 to 500 eggs.

words.definitionEn: a type of literature that describes imaginary people and events, not real ones
words.exampleEn: a work of popular fiction

words.definitionEn: an area of land in the country used for growing crops or keeping animals in, usually surrounded by a fence, etc.
words.exampleEn: We had to walk across a ploughed field.

words.definitionEn: a struggle against somebody/something using physical force
words.exampleEn: a street/gang fight

words.definitionEn: to take part in a war or battle against an enemy
words.exampleEn: soldiers trained to fight

words.definitionEn: a number representing a particular amount, especially one given in official information
words.exampleEn: the latest sales/crime/unemployment figures

words.definitionEn: to make a film of a story or a real event; to record somebody/something on film
words.exampleEn: They are filming in Moscow right now.

words.definitionEn: the last of a series of games or competitions, in which the winner is decided
words.exampleEn: She reached the final of the 100m hurdles.

words.definitionEn: after a long time, especially when there has been some difficulty or delay
words.exampleEn: The performance finally started half an hour late.

words.definitionEn: one of the four long thin parts that stick out from the hand (or five, if the thumb is included)
words.exampleEn: She ran her fingers through her hair.

words.definitionEn: the last part or the end of something
words.exampleEn: It was a close finish as they had predicted.

words.definitionEn: the person or thing that comes or happens before all other similar people or things
words.exampleEn: I was the first in my family to go to college.

words.definitionEn: used to introduce the first of a list of points you want to make in a speech or piece of writing
words.exampleEn: There are two reasons for this decision: firstly…

words.definitionEn: to try to catch fish with nets, a fishing line, etc.
words.exampleEn: The trawler was fishing off the coast of Iceland.

words.definitionEn: the sport or business of catching fish
words.exampleEn: They often go fishing.

words.definitionEn: healthy and strong, especially because you do regular physical exercise
words.exampleEn: Top athletes have to be very fit.

words.definitionEn: to be the right shape and size for somebody/something
words.exampleEn: I tried the dress on but it didn't fit.

words.definitionEn: to repair or correct something
words.exampleEn: The car won't start—can you fix it?

words.definitionEn: having a level surface, not curved or sloping, and without holes or any bits sticking out
words.exampleEn: low buildings with flat roofs

words.definitionEn: a serious disease caused by a virus, that causes a high temperature, severe pains and weakness
words.exampleEn: The whole family has the flu.

words.definitionEn: a small flying insect with two wings. There are many different types of fly and many of them spread disease.
words.exampleEn: A fly was buzzing against the window.

words.definitionEn: able to fly
words.exampleEn: flying insects

words.definitionEn: travelling in an aircraft
words.exampleEn: I'm terrified of flying.

words.definitionEn: the thing or person that people are most interested in; the act of paying special attention to something and making people interested in it
words.exampleEn: focus of something, It was the main focus of attention at the meeting.

words.definitionEn: to give attention, effort, etc. to one particular subject, situation or person rather than another
words.exampleEn: She was distracted and finding it hard to focus.

words.definitionEn: that is/are going to be mentioned next
words.exampleEn: Answer the following questions.

words.definitionEn: surrounded by somebody/something; in the middle of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: a house among the trees

words.definitionEn: a quantity of something
words.exampleEn: I was amazed at the amount he could eat.

words.definitionEn: in or from a country that is not your own
words.exampleEn: What foreign languages do you speak?

words.definitionEn: a large area of land that is thickly covered with trees
words.exampleEn: a tropical/pine forest

words.definitionEn: a tool with a handle and three or four sharp points (called prongs), used for picking up and eating food
words.exampleEn: to eat with a knife and fork

words.definitionEn: very correct and suitable for official or important occasions
words.exampleEn: ladies in formal evening wear

words.definitionEn: by good luck
words.exampleEn: I was late, but fortunately the meeting hadn't started.

words.definitionEn: towards a place or position that is in front
words.exampleEn: She leaned forward and kissed him on the cheek.

words.definitionEn: without payment
words.exampleEn: Children under five travel free.

words.definitionEn: recently produced or picked and not frozen, dried or preserved in tins or cans
words.exampleEn: Is this milk fresh?

words.definitionEn: a piece of electrical equipment in which food is kept cold so that it stays fresh
words.exampleEn: The kids tend to raid the fridge when they get home from school.

words.definitionEn: a small animal with smooth skin, that lives both on land and in water (= is an amphibian). Frogs have very long back legs for jumping, and no tail.
words.exampleEn: the croaking of frogs

words.definitionEn: that you enjoy
words.exampleEn: She's really fun to be with.

words.definitionEn: belonging to a period of history that is thousands of years in the past
words.exampleEn: Archaeologists are excavating the ruined temples of this ancient civilization.

words.definitionEn: objects that can be moved, such as tables, chairs and beds, that are put into a house or an office to make it suitable for living or working in
words.exampleEn: garden/office furniture

words.definitionEn: more; additional
words.exampleEn: Cook for a further 2 minutes.

words.definitionEn: taking place or existing at a time after the present
words.exampleEn: The land will remain untouched for future generations to enjoy.

words.definitionEn: a room or building for showing works of art, especially to the public
words.exampleEn: a picture gallery

words.definitionEn: a space where something is missing
words.exampleEn: Extra funding is needed to plug the gap.

words.definitionEn: any substance like air that is neither a solid nor a liquid, for example hydrogen or oxygen
words.exampleEn: Air is a mixture of gases.

words.definitionEn: a barrier like a door that is used to close an opening in a fence or a wall outside a building
words.exampleEn: the front/main gate

words.definitionEn: affecting all or most people, places or things
words.exampleEn: books of general interest (= of interest to most people)

words.definitionEn: a thing that you give to somebody, especially on a special occasion or to say thank you
words.exampleEn: to give/receive a gift

words.definitionEn: a frame with a net into which players must kick or hit the ball in order to score a point
words.exampleEn: He headed the ball into an open goal (= one that had nobody defending it).

words.definitionEn: the being or spirit that is worshipped and is believed to have created the universe
words.exampleEn: Do you believe in God?

words.definitionEn: bright yellow in colour, like gold
words.exampleEn: The company name was spelled out in gold letters.

words.definitionEn: a chemical element. Gold is a yellow precious metal used for making coins, jewellery, beautiful objects, etc.
words.exampleEn: gold coins/jewellery

words.definitionEn: a game played over a large area of ground using sticks with a special shape to hit a small hard ball (a golf ball) into a series of 9 or 18 holes, using as few shots as possible
words.exampleEn: I play golf every weekend.

words.definitionEn: behaviour that is morally right or acceptable
words.exampleEn: Do they even know the difference between good and evil?

words.definitionEn: the joint connecting the foot to the leg; the narrow part of the leg just above the ankle joint
words.exampleEn: to sprain/break/twist your ankle

words.definitionEn: the group of people who are responsible for controlling a country or a state
words.exampleEn: to lead/form a government

words.definitionEn: a common wild plant with narrow green leaves and stems that are eaten by cows, horses, sheep, etc.
words.exampleEn: a blade of grass

words.definitionEn: to say hello to somebody or to welcome them
words.exampleEn: greet somebody, He greeted all the guests warmly as they arrived.

words.definitionEn: the solid surface of the earth
words.exampleEn: on the ground, I found her lying on the ground.

words.definitionEn: a person that you have invited to your house or to a particular event that you are paying for
words.exampleEn: They only use the dining room when they have guests.

words.definitionEn: a person who shows other people the way to a place, especially somebody employed to show tourists around interesting places
words.exampleEn: Our tour guide showed us around the old town.

words.definitionEn: to show somebody the way to a place, often by going with them; to show somebody a place that you know well
words.exampleEn: guide somebody, It's a dangerous route—you'll need someone to guide you.

words.definitionEn: a weapon that is used for firing bullets or shells
words.exampleEn: I have never fired a gun in my life.

words.definitionEn: a man
words.exampleEn: a big/little guy

words.definitionEn: a thing that you do often and almost without thinking, especially something that is hard to stop doing
words.exampleEn: You need to change your eating habits.

words.definitionEn: to the extent of half
words.exampleEn: The glass was half full.

words.definitionEn: a space or passage inside the entrance or front door of a building
words.exampleEn: in a hall, Her brother was standing in the front hall.

words.definitionEn: in a cheerful way; with feelings of pleasure
words.exampleEn: children playing happily on the beach

words.definitionEn: used with the past participle to form perfect tenses
words.exampleEn: I've finished my work.

words.definitionEn: a continuous pain in the head
words.exampleEn: He had a terrible headache.

words.definitionEn: the organ in the chest that sends blood around the body, usually on the left in humans
words.exampleEn: The patient's heart stopped beating for a few seconds.

words.definitionEn: the quality of being hot
words.exampleEn: Heat rises.

words.definitionEn: to make something hot or warm; to become hot or warm
words.exampleEn: heat (something), Heat the oil and add the onions.

words.definitionEn: weighing a lot; difficult to lift or move
words.exampleEn: a heavy weight/load

words.definitionEn: the measurement of how tall a person or thing is
words.exampleEn: Height: 210 mm. Width: 57 mm. Length: 170 mm.

words.definitionEn: able to improve a particular situation
words.exampleEn: helpful advice/information

words.definitionEn: a person who is admired by many people for doing something brave or good
words.exampleEn: a war hero (= somebody who was very brave during a war)

words.definitionEn: of or belonging to her
words.exampleEn: His eyes met hers.

words.definitionEn: used when the woman or girl who performs an action is also affected by it
words.exampleEn: She hurt herself.

words.definitionEn: to put or keep somebody/something in a place where they/it cannot be seen or found
words.exampleEn: hide somebody/something + adv./prep., He hid the letter in a drawer.

words.definitionEn: at or to a position or level that is a long way up from the ground or from the bottom
words.exampleEn: to climb/jump/leap high

words.definitionEn: an area of land that is higher than the land around it, but not as high as a mountain
words.exampleEn: a region of gently rolling hills

words.definitionEn: used to emphasize an adjective or adverb in negative sentences or questions, meaning ‘at all’
words.exampleEn: He wasn't any good at French.

words.definitionEn: used when the man or boy who performs an action is also affected by it
words.exampleEn: He introduced himself.

words.definitionEn: of or belonging to him
words.exampleEn: He took my hand in his.

words.definitionEn: an act of hitting somebody/something with your hand or with an object held in your hand
words.exampleEn: Give it a good hit.

words.definitionEn: to bring your hand, or an object you are holding, against somebody/something quickly and with force
words.exampleEn: hit somebody/something, I was afraid he was going to hit me.

words.definitionEn: a game played on a field by two teams of 11 players, with curved sticks and a small hard ball. Teams try to hit the ball into the other team’s goal.
words.exampleEn: to play hockey

words.definitionEn: to have somebody/something in your hand, arms, etc.
words.exampleEn: hold somebody/something, She was holding a large box.

words.definitionEn: a hollow space in something solid or in the surface of something
words.exampleEn: He dug a deep hole in the garden.

words.definitionEn: connected with the place where you live
words.exampleEn: home life (= with your family)
